segments.text: Is not man consigned to labor on earth? Are not his days like those of a hired hand?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Like a slave he longs for shade; like a hireling he waits for his wages.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: So I am allotted months of futility, and nights of misery are appointed me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I lie down I think:‘When will I get up?’But the night drags on, and I toss and turn until dawn.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
My flesh is clothed with worms and encrusted with dirt;
my skin is cracked and festering.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My days are swifter than a weaver’s shuttle; they come to an end without hope.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Remember that my life is but a breath. My eyes will never again see happiness.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The eye that beholds me will no longer see me. You will look for me, but I will be no more.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: As a cloud vanishes and is gone, so he who goes down to Sheol does not come back up.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He never returns to his house; his place remembers him no more.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text:
Therefore I will not restrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit;
I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Am I the sea, or the monster of the deep, that You must keep me under guard?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: When I think my bed will comfort me and my couch will ease my complaint,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: then You frighten me with dreams and terrify me with visions,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: so that I would prefer strangling and deathover my life in this body.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I loathe my life! I would not live forever. Leave me alone, for my days are but a breath.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: What is man that You should exalt him, that You should set Your heart upon him,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: that You attend to him every morning, and test him every moment?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will You never look away from me, or leave me alone to swallow my spittle?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I have sinned, what have I done to You, O watcher of mankind? Why have You made me Your target, so that I am a burden to You?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Why do You not pardon my transgression and take away my iniquity? For soon I will lie down in the dust; You will seek me, but I will be no more.
segments.speaker: Job
